text: The patient is positioned in left lateral decubitus, and the abdomen is accessed with a varus needle for insufflation. Two additional ports are placed in the right abdomen under direct visualization. The da Vinci robot is docked, and the camera is inserted through an 8mm mid-abdominal port. The surgeon begins retroperitoneal dissection using blunt and sharp techniques along with electrocautery, entering the peritoneum near the liver. The hernia sac and retroperitoneal fat are identified, with previous mesh visible inferiorly and posteriorly. The surgeon dissects around the hernia sac, removes the previous mesh, and fully reduces the hernia sac contents into the abdomen.

The surgeon closes the hernia defect with a running O stratific suture, reinforced with interrupted PDS sutures. The defect is measured, and a twelve by twelve piece of mesh with rounded corners is aligned to cover the defect. The superior lip of the mesh is fixated using a running stratifix suture. The excised mesh is removed through a port site using an endo-catch bag, and fascial stitches are placed for later closure.

The mesh is visibly fixated on the superior edge, overlying the closed hernia defect. Fiber and glue are applied to the mesh to secure its fixation to the abdominal wall. The flap is closed using a running O stratifix suture.
